active_answer: when wrapping the leg straight, folding it afterward may cause compression on the limb
active_question: What compression warning does Rope365 give when wrapping a leg while it is straight?
edited_answer: Folding the leg after wrapping it straight can compress the limb, so the fit in the straight position should not be assumed to remain unchanged.
edited_question: Why should rope fit be rechecked if a wrapped straight leg is later folded?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement turns a dependent-clause fragment into the evidence's practical warning that a position change can alter limb compression.
reason: The replacement turns a dependent-clause fragment into the evidence's practical warning that a position change can alter limb compression.
support_evidence: | Day 137: Stockings – Wrap the legs without attaching them to anything. This can be decorative or used as a structure to tie other parts of the body. Take note that when wrapping the leg straight, folding it afterward may cause compression on the limb. Inspirations and Resources |

active_answer: Communication is key and consent is particularly important with this kind of play.
active_question: What does Rope365 emphasize about communication and consent in pressure-point play?
edited_answer: A spot may tickle one person, do nothing for another, or be extremely painful for someone else, so test intended spots first, learn that partner's responses, communicate, and confirm consent.
edited_question: Why does Rope365 recommend testing intended pressure spots with each partner before combining them with rope?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement preserves the consent guidance while restoring the source's concrete reason for partner-specific testing: responses to the same spot vary widely.
reason: The replacement preserves the consent guidance while restoring the source's concrete reason for partner-specific testing: responses to the same spot vary widely.
support_evidence: Every person is different, a spot that tickles for someone might do nothing for another, and be extremely painful for someone else. Some may even have pressure points that no one else has. It is best to try the different spots you aim to use before combining them with rope to measure the individual specificities and limit awkward surprise. Communication is key and consent is particularly important with this kind of play.

active_answer: to work loose when not under load (or under cyclic loading), to slip when pulled sideways, and the bight portion of the knot to capsize in certain circumstances
active_question: Which deficiencies can make a common bowline unsuitable for safety-critical use?
edited_answer: It can work loose without steady load or under cyclic loading, slip under a sideways pull, and have its bight capsize in some circumstances.
edited_question: Which failure modes can make a common bowline unsuitable for safety-critical use?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement converts the copied infinitive list into a grammatical, self-contained account of the same three failure modes.
reason: The replacement converts the copied infinitive list into a grammatical, self-contained account of the same three failure modes.
support_evidence: Although the bowline is generally considered a reliable knot, its main deficiencies are a tendency to work loose when not under load (or under cyclic loading), to slip when pulled sideways, and the bight portion of the knot to capsize in certain circumstances. To address these shortcomings, a number of more secure variations of the bowline have been developed for use in safety-critical applications, or by securing the knot with an overhand knot backup.

active_answer: it moves the rope away from the clavicles and makes an angle that creates more natural pressure on the body
active_question: Why does Rope365 say the Open V chest harness can be more comfortable?
edited_answer: Opening the front stem moves rope away from the clavicles and changes the angle at which it presses on the body, which the page says can feel more comfortable.
edited_question: What placement change does Rope365 say may make the Open V chest harness more comfortable?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement preserves the source's qualified comfort claim while expressing the observable rope-placement and pressure-angle changes instead of the vague phrase 'natural pressure'.
reason: The replacement preserves the source's qualified comfort claim while expressing the observable rope-placement and pressure-angle changes instead of the vague phrase 'natural pressure'.
support_evidence: We can open the front stem to shape the chest in a different way. This variation can be more comfortable as it moves the rope away from the clavicles and makes an angle that creates more natural pressure on the body.
